computation_name: Disk-Backed Vector ANN and Similarity Join
definition: Store a vector index or vector-join structure on SSD/disk and optimize page layout, caching, and probabilistic pruning so SPECTRA can retrieve top-k or thresholded train-eval vector similarities when the corpus exceeds RAM.

scale_profile.best_for: Billion-scale or memory-constrained vector audits where in-memory FAISS/HNSW is not feasible.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Billion-scale dense-vector collections in disk-optimized systems.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The dataset fits in memory; in-memory ANN or exact BLAS is simpler and easier to validate.

tunable_parameters.name: page_size_or_layout
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls disk I/O granularity and locality.

tunable_parameters.name: cache_budget
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls memory used for hot vectors or graph nodes.

quality_gates: Measure recall@k against exact or in-memory search on a subset.
Report index version, storage layout, and cache budget.
Check reproducibility because disk-resident indexes may update asynchronously.
failure_modes: I/O layout can dominate runtime and make results hardware-dependent.
OOD queries can have lower recall if the index assumes in-distribution routing.
Approximate pruning can bias hard SPECTRA bins.
example_usage: Use dense_vector_topk_similarity.py as a small exact reference; disk-backed systems should export the same SPECTRA pairwise CSV after querying the SSD-resident index.

citations.title: SPANN: Highly-efficient Billion-scale Approximate Nearest Neighbor Search
citations.evidence: Introduces a memory-disk hybrid system for billion-scale approximate nearest-neighbor search.

citations.title: DiskJoin: Large-scale Vector Similarity Join with SSD
citations.evidence: Introduces a disk-based algorithm for billion-scale vector similarity joins using I/O optimization, memory caching, and probabilistic pruning.
